scenario_description: A user tracks a specific blue cookbook titled 'Savory Sundays' as new books are added to the left of a shelf, pushing the row rightward until the original book falls off-screen and is replaced by a different book in the same visual position.

turns.text: Check out this corner of my shelf. I'm looking for that blue one on the far right edge.
turns.image_description: A close-up shot of a wooden bookshelf. From left to right: A thick red hardcover with gold lettering (Title: 'History of Art'), a slim black paperback (Title: 'Night Watch'), and a bright blue hardcover standing upright at the very right edge of the frame. The spine of the blue book clearly reads 'Savory Sundays'. There are no other books visible to the right of it; the shelf ends or the crop cuts off immediately after.

turns.text: Got it. That blue 'Savory Sundays' is definitely the one you're pointing out. It's sitting right next to the black 'Night Watch' on your right.
Added a couple more to the left side just now. Here's the update.
turns.image_description: The same shelf view, but the camera has panned slightly right to accommodate new additions. From left to right: A green hardcover ('Gardening 101') is now at the far left, followed by the red 'History of Art', then the black 'Night Watch'. To the right of the black book is the blue 'Savory Sundays'. Crucially, a new grey hardcover ('Modern Architecture') is now visible to the immediate right of the blue book, pushing the blue book further into the center-right of the frame.

turns.text: Looks like the row got crowded! The blue one moved over a bit to make room for that new grey book on its right.
Yeah, kept adding more. Now the blue one is gone from this spot.
turns.image_description: The shelf view again, panned further right. The sequence from left to right is: Green 'Gardening 101', Red 'History of Art', Black 'Night Watch', Grey 'Modern Architecture'. At the far right edge, where the blue book used to be relative to the grey one, there is now a completely different book: a yellow paperback titled 'Quick Bites'. The blue 'Savory Sundays' is no longer visible in the frame.

turns.text: Whoa, the blue one must have fallen off the end or been pushed out of frame entirely. That yellow 'Quick Bites' is taking its place now.
I need to know exactly how many books were originally to the left of 'Savory Sundays' before it disappeared. Was it A) 0, B) 1, C) 2, or D) 3?

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: In Turn 1, the image shows the blue 'Savory Sundays' at the far right. To its immediate left are two distinct books: the black 'Night Watch' and the red 'History of Art'. No other books are visible to the left of the red one in that initial frame. Therefore, there were exactly 2 books to the left of the target book. In subsequent turns, new books (green, grey) are added to the left, changing the count, but the question asks for the 'original' count before the displacement occurred. The final image shows the blue book missing and replaced by a yellow one, confirming the displacement scenario.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must distinguish between the state of the shelf in the first turn versus later turns. A model focusing only on the final image would see 4 books to the left of the yellow book (or 3 if counting differently) and fail. A model focusing on Turn 3 might count 3 books to the left of the blue book (Green, Red, Black). Only by correctly identifying the 'original' state in Turn 1 and counting the entities present *at that time* can the correct answer (2) be derived.

_meta.scenario: A user identifies a specific blue cookbook titled 'Savory Sundays' at the far right edge. Over three turns, books are added one by one to the left side of the shelf, pushing the entire row rightward until the cookbook falls off the visible frame and is replaced by a new book in that spot. The final question asks how many books were originally to the left of the cookbook before it disappeared.
